preamble: The shift from the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] to the voiced pharyngeal fricative [ʕ] (represented here as 'ain') is a significant historical sound change, notably occurring in the Semitic language family and proposed for the development of 'Nuclear' Proto-Indo-European.

examples.note: Historical merger of the uvular and pharyngeal phonemes into a single pharyngeal category in most Northwest Semitic languages.
Proposed shift of laryngeal consonants from uvular to pharyngeal articulation in the ancestor of non-Anatolian branches (Weiss, 2016).

sources: Weiss, M. (2016). The phonology of the Indo-European laryngeals.
